basics.description: **Note:** Please refer to the following before installing the solution: • Review the solution [Release Notes](https://github.com/Azure/Azure-Sentinel/tree/master/Solutions/UniFi%20Site%20Manager%20%28CCF%29/ReleaseNotes.md) • There may be [known issues](https://aka.ms/sentinelsolutionsknownissues) pertaining to this Solution, please refer to them before installing. The [UniFi Site Manager](https://unifi.ui.com/) solution for Microsoft Sentinel provides cloud-side telemetry ingestion via the [Site Manager API](https://developer.ui.com/site-manager-api/) for sites, devices, hosts and ISP metrics. Ships analytics rules covering ISP downtime, WAN issues, IPS/IDS posture, firmware drift, device offline events, configuration changes and security signals, plus an operations workbook for at-a-glance estate health. **Data Connector:** UniFi Site Manager (CCF) — single Connect deploys 4 polling rules with a single API key. **Underlying API tier:** the Site Manager API is available on all UniFi cloud plans. The Audit log endpoint requires Pro+; this solution does not depend on it. **Pre-requisites:** A UniFi Site Manager API key is required. Generate one at https://unifi.ui.com/api. **Data Connectors:** 1, **Workbooks:** 1, **Analytic Rules:** 21, **Hunting Queries:** 8 [Learn more about Microsoft Sentinel](https://aka.ms/azuresentinel) | [Learn more about Solutions](https://aka.ms/azuresentinelsolutionsdoc)

basics.toolTip: This filters by workspaces that exist in the Resource Group selected

basics.name: workspace
basics.label: Workspace
basics.toolTip: This dropdown will list only workspace that exists in the Resource Group selected

options.text: This Solution installs the data connector for UniFi Site Manager (CCF). You can get UniFi Site Manager (CCF) data in your Microsoft Sentinel workspace. After installing the solution, configure and enable this data connector by following guidance in Manage solution view.

link.label: Learn more about connecting data sources

options.text: This solution installs workbook(s) to help you gain insights into the telemetry collected in Microsoft Sentinel. After installing the solution, start using the workbook in Manage solution view.

elements.name: workbook1
elements.label: UniFi Site Manager Network Dashboard

options.text: The UniFi Site Manager workbook provides comprehensive visibility into your UniFi network infrastructure. Monitor device status, host connections, site health, and ISP performance metrics across all your UniFi deployments.
This solution installs the following analytic rule templates. After installing the solution, create and enable analytic rules in Manage solution view.

elements.name: analytic1
elements.label: UniFi Site Manager: Controller Connection State Change

options.text: Identifies when a UniFi controller (Cloud Key, Dream Machine, etc.) experiences a connection state change, which may indicate a network outage, power loss, or device failure.

elements.name: analytic2
elements.label: UniFi Site Manager: Data Connector Health

options.text: Identifies when UniFi Site Manager data connectors stop sending data, ensuring visibility into the UniFi estate is maintained.

elements.name: analytic3
elements.label: UniFi Site Manager: Device Offline

options.text: Identifies when a UniFi device transitions to offline status, which may indicate a network outage, power loss, or hardware failure requiring attention.

elements.name: analytic4
elements.label: UniFi Site Manager: External WAN IP changed

options.text: Identifies when a site reports more than one distinct WAN external IP within an hour, which may indicate ISP DHCP refresh, WAN reconfiguration, or routing hijack.

elements.name: analytic5
elements.label: UniFi Site Manager: Firmware Update Available

options.text: Identifies UniFi devices with firmware updates available. Keeping firmware patched is critical as updates often include security vulnerability fixes.

elements.name: analytic6
elements.label: UniFi Site Manager: IPS/IDS disabled or misconfigured

options.text: Identifies when a UniFi gateway threat-protection state transitions away from the expected IPS-active mode, which may indicate admin action or attacker tampering.

elements.name: analytic7
elements.label: UniFi Site Manager: IPS signature count dropped >50%

options.text: Identifies when the IPS signature ruleset count drops by more than half versus the 7-day maximum, indicating broken threat-intel feeds or rollback.

elements.name: analytic8
elements.label: UniFi Site Manager: ISP Downtime

options.text: Identifies when the ISP connection experiences downtime, impacting business operations and requiring documentation for SLA purposes.

elements.name: analytic9
elements.label: UniFi Site Manager: ISP High Latency

options.text: Identifies when ISP latency exceeds acceptable thresholds, impacting VoIP quality, video conferencing, and overall application performance.

elements.name: analytic10
elements.label: UniFi Site Manager: ISP Packet Loss

options.text: Identifies when WAN packet loss occurs. Even small loss percentages can significantly degrade VoIP calls and video conferencing quality.

elements.name: analytic11
elements.label: UniFi Site Manager: ISP SLA Breach

options.text: Identifies when ISP uptime falls below the SLA threshold. Useful for tracking SLA compliance and supporting ISP accountability conversations.

elements.name: analytic12
elements.label: UniFi Site Manager: Multiple Devices Offline

options.text: Identifies when multiple UniFi devices go offline simultaneously, typically signaling a power outage, upstream connectivity failure, or infrastructure issue requiring investigation.

elements.name: analytic13
elements.label: UniFi Site Manager: New Device Adopted

options.text: Identifies when a new device is adopted into the UniFi network. While often legitimate, unexpected adoptions may indicate unauthorized hardware additions.

elements.name: analytic14
elements.label: UniFi Site Manager: New WAN2 (secondary) issue recorded

options.text: Identifies a new issue index on the secondary WAN failover link. Fires only on newly-recorded issue indexes, not while existing issues persist.

elements.name: analytic15
elements.label: UniFi Site Manager: New WAN issue index recorded

options.text: Identifies a newly-recorded UniFi WAN issue index. May indicate ISP problems, hardware fault, or active denial-of-service pressure on the gateway.

elements.name: analytic16
elements.label: UniFi Site Manager: New critical notifications appeared

options.text: Identifies when the UniFi critical-notification count increases since the previous poll, signaling at least one new critical-severity alarm has appeared.

elements.name: analytic17
elements.label: UniFi Site Manager: Pending firmware updates outstanding for 7d+

options.text: Identifies sites with pending firmware updates outstanding continuously for the last 7 days, indicating patch debt that should be scheduled.

elements.name: analytic18
elements.label: UniFi Site Manager: Site Health Critical

options.text: Identifies when a site has multiple offline devices, indicating significant site-level issues such as a power outage, network failure, or infrastructure problem.

elements.name: analytic19
elements.label: UniFi Site Manager: System log shipping disabled

options.text: Identifies when a UniFi host transitions cloudSystemLogState from enabled to disabled. This defense-evasion signal may indicate an attacker silencing audit trails.

elements.name: analytic20
elements.label: UniFi Site Manager: WAN uptime below 99%

options.text: Identifies when site WAN uptime falls below 99 percent, an SLA-grade availability problem. Combine with the ISP Downtime rule for root-cause attribution.

elements.name: analytic21
elements.label: UniFi Site Manager: WiFi quality degraded (high TX retry)

options.text: Identifies when the site-wide WiFi TX retry rate exceeds 5 percent, which may indicate RF interference, channel congestion, or active wireless attack.
This solution installs the following hunting queries. After installing the solution, run these hunting queries to hunt for threats in Manage solution view.

elements.name: huntingquery1
elements.label: UniFi Site Manager: Console group membership churn

options.text: Tracks members of `userData.consoleGroupMembers` over time. Additions indicate new admin access; removals indicate revoked access. Both warrant review - additions are an account-creation signal, removals can be a sign of attacker cleanup. This hunting query depends on UniFiSiteManagerConnectorDefinition data connector (Unifi_SiteManager_Hosts_CL Parser or Table)

elements.name: huntingquery2
elements.label: UniFi Site Manager: Devices flapping online/offline

options.text: Devices that transitioned between online and offline more than 4 times in 24 hours. Flapping can indicate intermittent connectivity, hardware failure, environmental issues, or active interference targeting the device. This hunting query depends on UniFiSiteManagerConnectorDefinition data connector (Unifi_SiteManager_Devices_CL Parser or Table)

elements.name: huntingquery3
elements.label: UniFi Site Manager: Firmware drift hotspots

options.text: Devices behind on firmware updates - ranked by product line and model. Identifies models where firmware drift is concentrated, indicating either a vendor-side delay or operational neglect. Each unpatched device is a CVE attack surface. This hunting query depends on UniFiSiteManagerConnectorDefinition data connector (Unifi_SiteManager_Devices_CL Parser or Table)

elements.name: huntingquery4
elements.label: UniFi Site Manager: Firmware version diversity within a model

options.text: Models running multiple distinct firmware versions across the estate. Indicates inconsistent patch management - some units are forgotten or excluded from update windows. This hunting query depends on UniFiSiteManagerConnectorDefinition data connector (Unifi_SiteManager_Devices_CL Parser or Table)

elements.name: huntingquery5
elements.label: UniFi Site Manager: Long-tail ISP latency hotspots (P95)

options.text: Sites where the P95 ISP latency is significantly above the median over the last 7 days. Helps identify sites suffering occasional but severe latency spikes that average-based monitoring misses. This hunting query depends on UniFiSiteManagerConnectorDefinition data connector (Unifi_SiteManager_ISPMetrics_CL Parser or Table)

elements.name: huntingquery6
elements.label: UniFi Site Manager: Devices adopted outside business hours

options.text: New devices adopted into a UniFi site outside business hours (10pm - 6am local time, or weekends). Legitimate admin work usually happens in hours; unattended adoption can be unauthorised hardware joining the network. This hunting query depends on UniFiSiteManagerConnectorDefinition data connector (Unifi_SiteManager_Devices_CL Parser or Table)

elements.name: huntingquery7
elements.label: UniFi Site Manager: Sites with persistent WAN issues

options.text: Sites that have recorded WAN issues in the majority of polls over the last 24 hours. Indicates chronic ISP problems versus transient blips - escalate to provider. This hunting query depends on UniFiSiteManagerConnectorDefinition data connector (Unifi_SiteManager_Sites_CL Parser or Table)

elements.name: huntingquery8
elements.label: UniFi Site Manager: WAN external IP geographic deviation

options.text: Sites where the WAN external IP changed ASN or ISP within the last 30 days. Routine DHCP renewal stays within the same ISP; an ASN/ISP change suggests provider switch, BGP hijack, or routing anomaly that warrants verification. This hunting query depends on UniFiSiteManagerConnectorDefinition data connector (Unifi_SiteManager_Sites_CL Parser or Table)
